'福岡県田川郡香春町大字香春733', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:香春神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11667602}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kawara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kawarajinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}{{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}Ōaza Kawara 733 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Toyobime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
{{ill|lt=Buzen Province|Q1047134|WD=Q1047134|1=Buzen Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}
Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10）
Oshihone Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（Jōwa 10）
Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|WD=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|WD=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|WD=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|WD=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|WD=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|WD=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|WD=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|WD=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|WD=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:豊前国の式内社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:渡来人|社かわらしんしや]] [[Category:赤染氏|社かわらしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

'福岡県田川郡香春町大字香春733', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:香春神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11667602}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kawara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kawarajinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}{{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}Ōaza Kawara 733 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Toyobime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
{{ill|lt=Buzen Province|Q1047134|WD=Q1047134|1=Buzen Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}
Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10）
Oshihone Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（Jōwa 10）
Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|WD=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|WD=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|WD=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|WD=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|WD=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|WD=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|WD=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|WD=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|WD=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:豊前国の式内社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:赤染氏|社かわらしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|WD=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|WD=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|WD=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|WD=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|WD=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|WD=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|WD=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|WD=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:豊前国の式内社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:赤染氏|社かわらしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

'福岡県田川郡香春町大字香春733', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:香春神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11667602}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kawara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kawarajinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}{{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}Ōaza Kawara 733 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Toyobime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
{{ill|lt=Buzen Province|Q1047134|WD=Q1047134|1=Buzen Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}
Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10）
Oshihone Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（Jōwa 10）
Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|WD=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|WD=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|WD=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|WD=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|WD=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|WD=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|WD=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|WD=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|WD=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:赤染氏|社かわらしんしや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10）
Oshihone Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（Jōwa 10）
Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|WD=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|WD=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|WD=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|WD=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|WD=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|WD=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|WD=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|WD=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|WD=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:Akazome Clan]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10）
Oshihone Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（Jōwa 10）
Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|WD=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|WD=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|WD=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|WD=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|WD=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|WD=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|WD=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|WD=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|WD=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|WD=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|WD=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|WD=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|WD=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|WD=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:Akazome Clan]]

Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|qid=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|qid=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|qid=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|qid=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|qid=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|qid=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|qid=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|qid=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|qid=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|qid=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|qid=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|qid=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|qid=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|qid=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|qid=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|qid=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|qid=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|qid=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|qid=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|qid=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:Akazome Clan]]

Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10）
Oshihone Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}（Jōwa 10）
Toyobika Shrine - {{ill|lt=Senior Second Rank|Q11123277|qid=Q11123277|1=Senior Second Rank}}（Ken'nin 1） |創建 = Unknown（the three shrines were merged and called Kawara-gū in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2） |本殿 = Shinmei-style {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Kawara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kawara Shrine'''（かわらじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawara|Q573632|qid=Q573632|1=Kawara, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Tagawa District|Q1156353|qid=Q1156353|1=Tagawa District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|Q123258|qid=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Overview === There are six shrines of Buzen Province listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; three of them are at Kawara Shrine{{Efn2|The remaining three are all within {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|qid=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}.}}. The three are Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine, Oshihone Shrine, and Toyobika Shrine, which originally stood on the summits of the three Kawara mountains (Ichinodake, Ninodake, and Sannodake){{Efn2|It is said that Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine was on Ichinodake, Oshihone Shrine on Ninodake, and Toyobika Shrine on Sannodake, though there are alternate theories.}}. In {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|qid=Q23514|1=709}}), the three summit shrines were moved to the current site, creating present-day Kawara Shrine. Since ancient times it stood, with {{ill|lt=Usa Jingū|Q715632|qid=Q715632|1=Usa Jingū}}, as one of Buzen's major shrines. Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme Shrine and Oshihone Shrine received {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} in {{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=843|Q23469|qid=Q23469|1=843}}), notably earlier than the dates when Nara's {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|qid=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (859), {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|qid=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} (868), and {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|qid=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}} (897) reached Senior First Rank. Kawara Shrine had very high status in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. While Buzen's ichinomiya is generally considered Usa Jingū, some old materials record Kawara Shrine as ichinomiyaYuzuru Miura, ''National Shrine Directory'', National Shrine Directory Publication Society. The priesthood has been successively held by the Akazome and Tsuruga families. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshihone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyobime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} The {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} identifies Toyobime-no-Mikoto with Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōme-no-Mikoto. An inscription before the shrine states: "First seat: Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto, who in the age of the gods crossed to Tang lands and returned in the reign of Emperor Sujin to be enshrined at the First Peak of Takawa District, Buzen. Second seat: Oshihone-no-Mikoto, child of Amatsuhi-Ōmikami, whose rough spirit manifested at the Second Peak. Third seat: Toyobime-no-Mikoto, maternal grandmother of Emperor Jinmu and mother of Sumiyoshi Ōmyōjin, enshrined at the Third Peak." In extant fragments of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buzen Fudoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto is described as a deity who came from Silla{{Cite book|和書|title=History of Japanese Buddhism|date=1944-11-30|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|page=261|author=Zennosuke Tsuji}}. The relation between Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} has long been discussed. Empress Jingū, mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, appears in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto / Ōtarashihime-no-Mikoto. "Karakuni" also resonates with Korea where she is linked in tradition to the {{ill|lt=Three Korean Conquests|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}. Some works identify the two as the same deityTsuneari Ito, ''Dazaikanai-shi'', Rekishi Tosho Sha. === Divine ranks === Karakuni-Okinaga-Ōhime-Ōjime Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Oshihone Shrine - Senior First Rank（Jōwa 10） Toyobika Shrine - Senior Second Rank（{{ill|lt=Ken'nin|Q1195433|qid=Q1195433|1=Kennin}} 1） === Crest === Triple-step lozenge; there is a theory this was granted by the {{ill|lt=Kokura Domain|Q1140943|qid=Q1140943|1=Kokura Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara clan|Q2975340|qid=Q2975340|1=Ogasawara clan}}. === Buildings === * Honden - Shinmei nagare-zukuri === Cultural properties === === Town-designated cultural properties === * Kawara Shrine documents and mandala * Kawara Shrine honden, haiden, east corridor, and stone wall === Main events === * {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Hitahikosan Line|Q5360616|qid=Q5360616|1=Hitahikosan Line}} {{ill|lt=Kawara Station|Q5360326|qid=Q5360326|1=Kawara Station}}: 15 minutes on foot (1.2 km) * {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|qid=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Kokura-minami Interchange|Q1575744|qid=Q1575744|1=Kokura-minami interchange}} 19 km; {{ill|lt=Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Q2648028|qid=Q2648028|1=Higashikyushu Expressway}} from {{ill|lt=Yukuhashi Interchange|Q2369745|qid=Q2369745|1=Yukuhashi interchange}} 13.5 km. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:香春町の建築物]] [[Category:香春町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Buzen Province]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:Akazome Clan]]
